doctor_turn: Hello, I’m Dr. Lee. What brought you in today?
patient_turn: Hi, um, I’ve got this really sore throat that started last night. It’s been getting worse, and it kinda burns when I swallow. I’m, uh, also feeling really tired and had some chills.

doctor_turn: When exactly did the sore throat start?
patient_turn: It started, um, last night, I think. I noticed it getting worse as the evening went on, and now it’s pretty painful.

doctor_turn: Where exactly do you feel the pain in your throat? Does it spread anywhere else?
patient_turn: It’s mostly in the back of my throat, like, really localized there. I don’t feel it spreading anywhere else, just, um, a lot of pain when I try to swallow.

doctor_turn: What does the pain feel like? Is it sharp, dull, burning, or something else?
patient_turn: It’s more of a burning sensation, honestly. Like, it really stings when I swallow, and it’s just, um, really uncomfortable.

doctor_turn: What makes the pain better or worse for you?
patient_turn: Well, it feels worse when I swallow, which is kind of tough because I've mostly been eating ice cream to soothe it. Also, I was trying to drink some warm tea, but I think I just ended up burning myself a bit. Oh, and I remember that I had a really bad cold a while ago, but I don't think this is related, right?

doctor_turn: What makes the pain better or worse for you?
patient_turn: Um, I think the pain is kind of, like, burning? But I'm not totally sure. It seems like it might get a little worse when I swallow, but I guess it could just be me overthinking it.

doctor_turn: Are you having trouble swallowing both liquids and solids?
patient_turn: Yeah, I’m having trouble with both, honestly. It really hurts to swallow anything, even liquids. I’ve mostly been eating ice cream because it’s, um, the only thing that feels okay going down.

doctor_turn: How has your voice changed since the pain started?
patient_turn: My voice has gotten pretty hoarse and raspy, um, and I’ve even lost some of it. It’s hard to talk without it hurting, so I’m trying not to say too much.

doctor_turn: Have you noticed any cough or runny nose along with your throat pain?
patient_turn: No, I haven’t noticed any cough or runny nose. It’s just the sore throat and, um, some mild abdominal soreness. I had a cold a couple of weeks ago, but this feels different.

doctor_turn: Can you describe the abdominal soreness you're feeling?
patient_turn: It’s just a mild, generalized soreness, um, nothing too intense. It started this morning, but it’s not really my main concern right now. I’m more focused on the throat pain, to be honest.

doctor_turn: How has the throat pain affected what you’re able to eat or drink?
patient_turn: It’s made it really hard to eat or drink anything, honestly. I’ve mostly been sticking to ice cream because it’s, um, cold and feels a bit soothing. I’m just not able to eat much else because it hurts too much to swallow.

doctor_turn: Did you have a fever when you arrived here?
patient_turn: Yeah, I did have a fever when I got here. I’ve been feeling chills and really fatigued since the throat pain started, so, um, I guess that makes sense.

doctor_turn: Have you had any trouble breathing or feeling short of breath?
patient_turn: No, I haven’t had any trouble breathing or feeling short of breath. It’s just the throat pain and, um, the fatigue that’s been bothering me. No respiratory issues at all.

doctor_turn: Yes, I had a cold about two weeks ago, but it was just a runny nose, nothing like this throat pain.
patient_turn: Right, exactly. The cold was just a runny nose, and I didn’t have any throat issues back then. This feels, um, really different and much worse.

doctor_turn: Has your girlfriend had a sore throat or any illness recently?
patient_turn: Yeah, she had a mild sore throat recently, but I haven’t seen her in several days. I’m not sure if that’s related to what I’m feeling, though.

doctor_turn: Have you had any known exposure to COVID-19 recently?
patient_turn: No, I haven’t had any known exposure to COVID-19. I’ve just been, um, staying home and attending my classes online, so I don’t think that’s a factor.

doctor_turn: Do you have any past medical or surgical history I should know about?
patient_turn: No, I don’t have any past medical or surgical history. I’m otherwise healthy, um, just dealing with this sore throat right now. No respiratory conditions or anything like that.

doctor_turn: Are you currently taking any medications or supplements?
patient_turn: No, I’m not taking any medications or supplements right now. I don’t have any allergies except for, um, mild seasonal rhinitis.

doctor_turn: Do you have any other allergies besides seasonal rhinitis?
patient_turn: No, it’s just the mild seasonal rhinitis. I don’t have any other allergies that I know of.

doctor_turn: Do you smoke or use any recreational drugs?
patient_turn: No, I don’t smoke, and I don’t use any recreational drugs. I just, um, drink alcohol socially on weekends, but that’s about it.

doctor_turn: How often do you drink alcohol on the weekends?
patient_turn: I usually drink socially on the weekends, um, maybe a couple of times, but I don’t really keep track. It’s just, you know, with friends sometimes.

doctor_turn: Who do you live with?
patient_turn: I live with a roommate, um, but we mostly keep to ourselves. It’s just been me dealing with this sore throat lately.

doctor_turn: How do you attend your classes? Are they online or in person?
patient_turn: I attend all my classes online. It’s been, um, pretty convenient, but I guess it’s also been a bit isolating, especially now that I’m feeling this way.
